per_turn_leaks.student_message: Let's focus on the episodes. Walk me through one from start to finish.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Okay... So it usually starts with a tightness in my chest that gets worse when I breathe in deeply or cough. Then, my airways start constricting and I feel like I'm wheezing, but it's not just a regular wheeze - it sounds more like a loud, raspy breath. At its worst, I'll get this sharp pain on the right side of my chest that makes me catch my breath even more. It's really uncomfortable and hard to relax through.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: During an episode, do you notice anything besides the main symptom?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yeah... sometimes during an episode, I'll feel a low-grade fever, but it's not always there. And occasionally, I'll get this feeling like my body is just burning up from the inside out - it's hard to explain, but it's almost like my skin is on fire or something. It's really uncomfortable and makes me feel even more miserable than usual.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Specifically, any changes in your skin — like sweating or flushing?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I think I might have mentioned that before, but yeah... sometimes at night, I'll wake up drenched in sweat. It's not just a little bit of sweat, either - it's like I've been pouring water all over my pajamas and they're completely soaked through. It's really weird and uncomfortable, to be honest.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: And your stomach — any queasiness or nausea during these moments?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, I haven't really experienced any nausea or queasiness during these episodes. But my appetite has definitely been off - it's like I just don't feel like eating anything at all, even when I'm hungry. The thought of food just seems overwhelming right now.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you had any fevers, chills, or night sweats?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I have been having low-grade fevers, around 38.2 degrees Celsius (100.8 Fahrenheit), and they seem to be worse in the evenings. As for chills or night sweats, I did mention earlier that I've been experiencing night sweats - it's like my body is just drenched in sweat every single night.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you noticed any changes in your weight or appetite?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I have been experiencing a decrease in appetite over the past 5 days, to be honest. It's like I just don't feel like eating anything at all, even when I'm hungry. The thought of food just seems overwhelming right now. As for weight changes, I haven't really noticed any significant differences, but that might be because I've been too tired to do much of anything except rest and try to recover from these episodes.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: And your diet — what does a typical day of eating look like?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, my appetite has been really off lately, so I haven't been eating much. Even when I do feel like eating, it's hard to make myself cook or prepare meals because everything just seems too overwhelming. I've mostly been surviving on coffee and toast or something simple like that.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Now family history. Any medical problems run in your family?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My mom has asthma, which is why I've always been a bit curious about my own respiratory health. But aside from that, I don't think there's anything else particularly notable in our family history. My grandma on my mom's side passed away from lung cancer, but that was many years ago when she was older.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Specifically, has anyone in your family had serious or chronic conditions?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My mom has asthma, which is probably why I've always been a bit curious about my own respiratory health. Other than that, I don't think there's anything else particularly notable in our family history. My grandma on my mom's side passed away from lung cancer, but that was many years ago when she was older.
